greetings online friends
as is tradition
i guess
mmm hmm
what else could it mean?
i think that's a sloth
i've yet to know a chick who was into daggers/knives
wow that looks awful
yes it is not friday
what does that even mean?
i'm sorry what
don't say harvested please
HELLO MR THOMPSON
brings me back
really kind of sums it up
hope they are better than the lemon party
lots of good things here
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: It's been a hell of a week...
item.description:
Monday morning my wife took herself to the hospital because she was leaking fluid, she got admitted for preterm labor, and possible preeclampsia. The doctors planned to keep her on magnesium and steroids and to stall the labor until Wednesday. Tuesday during an ultrasound they find a huge mass in my son's chest that is squishing his heart and lungs (Google CPAM if you're curious). I miraculously finished my training at work early and arrived at the hospital shortly after they found out and I learned the news. Within half an hour my wife and I were being life flighted out of state to a hospital with a pediatric surgeon who deals with this issue regularly.
The flight was about an hour. Luckily it was in a fixed wing plane so no helicopter. It was still super cramped.
The plane is fully equipped with all the necessary life support and they were checking our baby's heart rate every 15 minutes.
Luckily my wife managed to get some sleep on the flight.
Pardon the selfie, but I did get to wear this nifty headset. Before we could land we had to wait for a passenger plane to make an emergency landing because they had an engine catch fire. Can you guess what kind of plane that was? If you guessed it was a Boeing, you're 100% correct!
My wife is a goddamn champion, and I couldn't be more proud of her. The continued with the medicine at the new hospital so she went through three days of off and on contractions while being on magnesium, having her vitals checked every few hours until they officially started an induction on Wednesday night to get us into active labor. All the while she gets no sleep and is still worried about me managing to get a decent amount of rest whenever I could. 17 more hours of active labor and our little boy was born at 5 lbs 1 oz.
We got to hold him for exactly one minute before I cut the cord and they passed him to the NICU.
Once he was stabilized they brought him over soy wife and I could see him before he was transported to the children's hospital across the way.
I was able to go with them but my wife was still recovering and had to wait. He spent Wednesday night and Thursday there getting around the clock care. Friday morning he went in for surgery. They removed the full upper lobe of his right lung where the mass was.
This was taken Friday after surgery. Everything went as well as we could have hoped. He is already improving so quickly and his heart lungs moved right back and the lungs inflated like they should. His vitals keep getting better and it looks like he's going to be a perfectly healthy boy eventually with no long lasting health issues. I haven't been able to hold him, and we won't be able to until he's off the ventilator, and that's likely not going to happen before I have to go back home for work. We're incredibly fortunate that my wife has maternity leave and FMLA so she'll be able to stay out here for the four to six weeks he'll need to stay in the NICU, and we got a room at the Ronald McDonald house so food and a place to sleep won't be an issue. I'll be able to stay out here for another week before I make the 6 hour drive back home. I'm dreading leaving them, my wife and I haven't been apart for more than a few days since we started dating, but we can't have lots of animals at home and with the medical costs of everything looming on the horizon, I can't afford to lose the job I just got. (For context I got laid off in March, normally I'm a Software and Data Engineer, but the job market has been a nightmare. Unemployment insurance ran out and I had to get a job delivering for Amazon to keep us afloat. I do have an interview next week for a super nice remote data job so fingers crossed the delivery gig doesn't last too long ) Anyway, this is probably going to be the longest month of my life. It's going to suck. However, I'm so grateful for all the care we've gotten, how fortunate we are that he's already recovering so well, so thankful that we have a place for my wife to stay for free while our boy is in the hospital, and talking with other families at the hospital, I know there's so much worse things that could happen to a child during development. One quick surgery and a few weeks in the NICU don't seem that bad by comparison. TLDR: son came early and needed surgery, but everything is going super well and he's going to make a full recovery
item.dc:creator: Necridos

item.title: 12/? Dr. Frankenfurter & Riff Raff
item.description: “I’m just a sweet Transvestite from Transexual Transylvania” 12/?? Dr. Frankenfurter & Riff Raff from Rocky Horror Picture Show I am doing a costume a day for the entire month and I am raising money for charity while doing so! Read more about it here :) https://imgur.com/gallery/see-y-all-tomorrow-GJKtKLD
item.dc:creator: josiemarcellino

item.title: Backlogged memes: I bet my battery dies uploading this
item.description:
My hero
They should do that for all questions. I’m curious and don’t like being judged.
It’s 1000$ don’t even look at it just move along just keep it moving it’s not even that pretty
Fall is an actual thing here and it’s lovely! This is a first. In Texas it’s still summer. But in Germany??? GLUHWEIN SEASON. (It doesn’t have any glue in it. It’s hot spiced wine and it’s awful but the Germans do insist and they know how to do Christmas RIGHT)
They’re gonna hurt your feelings. Guaranteed.
This person gets it. My husband wants to make like a Soviet space program control board for our son and he was like talk me out of it and I was like no way that sounds awesome
There really is a lot of time in the day and I’m mad at former me for not writing more. Depressed ass bitch. What do you mean you’re not “feeling inspired” open your goddamn laptop
@CodyBurkett this one was either made for you or made BY you
It’s why I get my very own duvet! My son takes it one step further and sleep crawls all over the place and ends up by my feet or curled up against my back or with his feet in my face. How can someone so small take up an entire bed.
I use wax actually it works rather well
Did you know if you ask for an Americano here they just give you a black coffee? In America it’s a whole ass drink with milk and stuff in it. This doesn’t say anything good about Americans does it
Gurl, same
I just really liked this artwork. This part always gets me
It’s true I would
That’s def the kind of ghost I wanna be when I die Speaking of, this spooky season I recommend The Blackening, Cabin in the Woods, and Paranorman, lately I have been in a horror comedy mood (paranorman is like horror for kids which is weird but I’m down for it, it’s such a cute movie, Coraline is also a good one of these) Genuinely scary recommendations are as always The Haunting of Hill House and Sinister, and if you’re exploring the Conjureverse the only good ones are Conjuring 1, Annabelle Comes Home, annabelle creation, and The Nun, the other ones are wastes of time, although I was appropriately creeped out by conjuring 2. I’m always taking recommendations also!
local man discovers that reality is unskippable, more at 11 (the premise of Click btw, but I do think the story would’ve hit harder if the remote hadn’t glitched, really the guy was taken advantage of by a bait and switch faulty bed bath and beyond product, it wasn’t predictable and yet inevitable due to his nature)
This is the B plot of my book. Assassin breaks in to kill a king but lets him live if he marries her and she becomes his spymaster but every once in awhile they have sex at knifepoint and he pretends like it’s a mild inconvenience but he’s secretly into it. He’s weird. Buuuut the main couple is still underage so it was our only option for some heat in book 1.
Oops Guess whose son is old enough now to show signs of autism…..I’m thrilled but also terrified send help
I mean this was back when whaling was a thing right? Why not let the guy hunt his whale? Cmon guys you could eat it after and everything
Oooooooooooooof Then again this was nearly ten years ago and nobody’s done fucking anything so I’m sure it’ll just get worse
Actually that first bit is very accurate from what I’ve heard, everyone forms a lovely little community. The father in law goes every year. Pretty sure the next camp over is all sex and drugs though
This deserves to be seen by the world
I love throwing stuff like this into books. Characters do tiny things like this and it makes them just instantly lovable. One of them does this exact thing with little kids’ clothes he’s my favorite
BRO TOOK THE WHOLE SQUAD DOWN
That or a prepubescent boy
My book is technically a hamlet retelling. Happened on accident, I watched a lot of lion king growing up
I’d so read this
I want it
And it’ll take me 500k when I write my novel about the gay vampire dads shush
I recommend this highly
I need this too. Maybe I just like wooden furniture
This is how I got the second cat in the first place
…and that is my legal defense your honor
Thrice now!
Or Anne. Or Leigh. Or Elizabeth.
It does seem like a lot of work
Oh good I needed a new hacksaw for my many crimes
P&P memes as per request Darcy is the most autistic mf in the universe
Oh, Darcy. A guy tried this line on me once (not like a quote, like he liked me despite me being not good enough) and we deeeefinitely did not get married.
She’d have you believe it’s a universal truth
Obligatory ad for my book. Trope cloud. You’ll like it. I ran out of advanced copies! A few imgurians got the last ones… they’re in a suitcase right now on a plane headed to America. All signed and stuff. Very exciting. The advanced copies are special and look different and are pretty
That’s my time, later dorks, stay dorky
